title: Seminar for students presenting first INTERGEN project results
paragraphs: A seminar for students presenting the first INTERGEN project results was organized at the Jan Kochanowski University in Kielce, Poland. Prof. UJK dr hab. Anna Wójcik Karpacz opened the seminar and introduced the guest.

Mr. Tomasz Molęda from Simpleoutsoursing.pl company, involved also as co-owner in the Education via Internet Association told students his entrepreneur story. He presented the business offer of his company which he opened when he was only 19 years old. He also told the students about the support programs he attended. At the beginning he based on the financial support of his family, later he gained organizational help from the Academic Incubator and now the company is located in Kielce Technology Park. He runs the company with his wife for 15 years. He shared experiences of how running the company together with the relative influence on their life, private sphere, and relations.

Then Ph.D. Joanna Rudawska presented the goals of the INTERGEN project, partnership, research methodology and the first results from gathered questionaires. The most interesting part for students was the comparison analysis between countries and the entrepreneurship attitude of Polish students, they willingness to stay in Poland and to run own company.

images.alt: Seminar for students presenting the first INTERGEN project results at Jan Kochanowski University in Kielce.
